title: Gymnolomia guatemalensis
intro: Gymnolomia guatemalensis is a plant species of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Guatemala mint. The leaves of Gymnolomia guatemalensis have been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Gymnolomia guatemalensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gymnolomia guatemalensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly prepared as remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
